description: Token consumption optimization — smart prune, prompt caching, tool trimming, memory tuning, log-viewer diagnostics

userStories.title: Log-viewer list API: add cache token fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the log-viewer list API to return cache_read_tokens and cache_write_tokens so that I can see caching behavior at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/query.ts list(), add cache_read_tokens and cache_write_tokens to the select from LlmLogTokensTable join
Update ListItem interface to include cache_read_tokens: number | null, cache_write_tokens: number | null
Existing list API response shape is a superset of before (backward compatible)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log-viewer detail API: add computed diagnostic fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I want detail API to include system_prompt_tokens, tool_count, and message_count so I can diagnose token waste per request.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/query.ts get(), after decompressing system_prompt, compute system_prompt_tokens = Math.round(Buffer.byteLength(systemPrompt, 'utf-8') / 4)
Compute tool_count from requestRow.tools (parse JSON, count array length or object keys)
Compute message_count from decompressed messages array length
Add these three fields to the Detail interface and return object
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log-viewer stats API: add total_tokens aggregation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the stats API to return total_tokens so I can see overall token consumption.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/query.ts stats(), add total_tokens to StatsSummary: total_input_tokens + total_output_tokens + total_reasoning_tokens + total_cache_read_tokens + total_cache_write_tokens
Update StatsSummary interface to include total_tokens: number
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log-viewer analyze API: add memory_model_cost and tool_count_high suggestions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want analyze to detect when memory agents use expensive models and when tool counts are too high.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/query.ts analyze(), after existing suggestions, add memory_model_cost check: query for agent in ('memory-extractor','memory-recall') and check if avg cost.input > 3.0 per MTok, if so add suggestion with category 'memory_model_cost'
Add tool_count_high check: query LlmLogToolCallTable grouped by llm_log_id, compute avg tool count, if > 30 add suggestion with category 'tool_count_high'
Both suggestions include description, impact, and recommended_action fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log-viewer analyze API: add category_no_model and prompt_prefix_unstable suggestions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want analyze to detect when task categories lack model config and when system prompt prefix is unstable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add category_no_model check: look for llm_log entries where variant contains a category name but the model matches the most common (primary) model, suggest configuring category-specific models
Add prompt_prefix_unstable check: if prompt_prefix_hash column exists in annotations or log metadata, check variation frequency within sessions, if > 50% changes suggest stabilizing prompt prefix
If prompt_prefix_hash data is not yet available (depends on US-008), skip this check gracefully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1 observability. prompt_prefix_unstable may be a no-op until US-008 lands.

userStories.title: Log-viewer stats: per-session cache hit rate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stats group_by=session to include cache_hit_rate so I can see caching effectiveness per conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/query.ts stats(), when group_by is 'session', add cache_hit_rate to each StatsGrouped entry
cache_hit_rate = coalesce(sum(cache_read_tokens), 0) / nullif(coalesce(sum(input_tokens), 0) + coalesce(sum(cache_read_tokens), 0) + coalesce(sum(cache_write_tokens), 0), 0)
Update StatsGrouped interface to include optional cache_hit_rate: number
Return 0 when denominator is 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1 observability. File: packages/opencode/src/log/query.ts stats() function

userStories.title: Stabilize system prompt prefix for cache hits
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the system prompt prefix to be stable across LLM calls so Anthropic's prefix-based caching maximizes hits.
acceptanceCriteria: In session/system.ts environment(), move the 'You are powered by the model named ...' line to the end of the <env> block, after Platform and Today's date
Add a code comment in memory/hooks/inject.ts explaining that priority 130 ensures memory is appended last in system prompt, which is critical for prefix caching stability
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record prompt_prefix_hash in log capture
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each LLM call to record a hash of the system prompt prefix so I can detect prompt instability in log-viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: In log/capture.ts pre-llm hook, compute prompt_prefix_hash = Bun.hash(systemPromptString.slice(0, 2000)).toString(16)
Store prompt_prefix_hash as an annotation on the llm_log entry with type 'prompt_prefix_hash' and content = the hash string
Only record when system prompt is non-empty
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Skip denied tools in resolveTools schema registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tools that are deny-only in agent permissions to not be registered so their JSON schema doesn't waste tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: In session/prompt.ts resolveTools(), before registering each tool, check if the agent's permission rules contain a deny rule for that tool with no 'ask' fallback
If the tool is fully denied, skip registering it (do not add to the tools object passed to the LLM)
Tools with 'ask' permission are still registered (user may approve at runtime)
The explore agent (which denies most tools via permission '*': 'deny' then allows specific ones) should only have its allowed tools registered
Verify by checking that explore agent's tool count is significantly less than sisyphus agent's tool count
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. File: packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts resolveTools() around line 818

userStories.title: Add tools_include whitelist to Agent config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to configure an explicit tool whitelist per agent so I can precisely control which tools are registered.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional tools_include: string[] to Agent.Info schema
In resolveTools(), if agent.tools_include is set, only register tools whose names are in the list
Support MCP server wildcard: 'lark-mcp/*' matches all tools from the lark-mcp server
When tools_include is not set, fall back to existing behavior (register all, then US-009 deny filtering)
Add tools_include to the agent config schema in config/config.ts so users can set it via config file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add retain field to ToolPart and prune timing adjustment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tool parts to carry a retain flag and prune to run before overflow checks so smart pruning can prevent expensive compaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional retain: boolean field to MessageV2.ToolPart type definition in session/message-v2.ts
Move the prune() call from prompt.ts:793 (agent stopped) to processor.ts just before the isOverflow check at line 283-288
Keep the existing prune() call at agent stopped as well (belt and suspenders)
Prune logic: when retain === false, always prune regardless of position; when retain === true, skip; when undefined, use existing position-based logic (PRUNE_PROTECT/PRUNE_MINIMUM)
Add prune log: log.info('pruned', { count, retained, saved_tokens })
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Core of smart prune. Files: packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts, packages/opencode/src/session/compaction.ts, packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts

userStories.title: Heuristic retain inference for unmarked tool outputs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a fallback heuristic that infers tool output importance when LLM doesn't mark retain, based on tool type and whether output was referenced.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a heuristic module in session/prune-heuristic.ts (or similar)
Default tool weights: read=0.8, grep=0.5, glob=0.4, bash=0.3 (configurable via config.compaction.tool_weights)
Reference check: scan the next assistant message's text parts for substrings from the tool output (file paths, variable names — first 5 significant tokens)
If weight > 0.5 OR output was referenced, set inferred retain = true; otherwise retain = false
Only runs for parts where retain === undefined
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Depends on US-011 for the retain field.

userStories.title: Prune hook chain for user-defined trimming
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prune hook so users can customize how tool outputs are trimmed, even for retained outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Register a new HookChain type 'prune' in session/hooks.ts (or wherever HookChain types are defined)
Before prune marks a part as compacted, execute the prune hook chain with the part as input
Hook receives: { part, retain, toolName, outputBytes } and can return: { retain: boolean, trimmedOutput?: string }
If hook returns trimmedOutput, update part.state.output with the trimmed content instead of fully compacting
Set a timeout of 100ms per hook call to prevent blocking
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Memory extraction frequency reduction and smart skip
userStories.description: As a developer, I want memory extraction to run less often and skip trivial rounds so background LLM calls are reduced.
acceptanceCriteria: In memory/hooks/auto-extract.ts, change DEFAULT_INTERVAL from 10 to read from config.memory.extract_interval (default 20)
Change MIN_MESSAGES from 4 to read from config.memory.extract_min_messages (default 6)
Add smart skip: count user text messages since last extraction, if < 2 skip this round
Log when skipped: log.info('extraction skipped', { reason, sessionID, userMsgCount })
Add config schema entries for extract_interval and extract_min_messages in config/config.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Recovery extraction: time limit and batching
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery extraction to be limited to recent sessions and process in batches to avoid startup overload.
acceptanceCriteria: In memory/hooks/auto-extract.ts recoveryExtract(), filter sessions to only those with time_created within config.memory.recovery_max_age_days (default 7) days
Process sessions in batches of 5: extract batch, await all, then next batch
Add config.memory.recovery_max_age_days to config schema
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add prompt_level field to Agent.Info
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt_level field on agents so system prompt injection can be tiered.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent/agent.ts, add prompt_level: 'full' | 'medium' | 'lite' to Agent.Info schema (optional, inferred from defaults)
Backward compat: if lite: true is set, treat as prompt_level: 'lite'
Default inference: mode === 'primary' → 'full'; mode === 'subagent' → 'medium'; lite: true → 'lite'
Set compaction agent to prompt_level: 'lite', title/summary agents to 'lite'
Set memory-extractor/memory-recall to 'lite' (already lite: true)
Allow user override via config.agent.<name>.prompt_level
Add prompt_level to agent config schema in config/config.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement prompt_level system prompt construction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want prompt.ts to use prompt_level when building the system prompt array so medium agents skip AGENTS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: In session/prompt.ts where system prompt is built (around line 720), replace the agent.lite ternary with a switch on agent.prompt_level
full: [...SystemPrompt.environment(model), ...InstructionPrompt.system()] (current non-lite behavior)
medium: [...SystemPrompt.environment(model)] (skip InstructionPrompt.system() — no AGENTS.md)
lite: [] (current lite behavior)
Memory injection hook still runs for full and medium (not lite) — verify inject.ts hook checks prompt_level !== 'lite'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3. Depends on US-016. File: packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts

userStories.title: Memory and category model usage warnings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to be warned when memory agents or task categories use my expensive primary model so I can configure cheaper alternatives.
acceptanceCriteria: In memory/engine/extractor.ts, when resolved model equals Provider.defaultModel(), emit log.warn('memory-extractor using primary model, consider config.agent.memory-extractor.model') on first call per session
In memory/engine/recall.ts, same warning for memory-recall

In tool/delegate-task.ts, when category is specified but has no model configured, emit log.warn('category X has no model, using primary. Configure via config.categories.X.model') on first use per session
Publish warnings via Bus.publish() so TUI can display them
Use a Set per session to ensure each warning type fires at most once
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compaction input slimming: stripSynthetic option
userStories.description: As a developer, I want compaction to skip synthetic text parts and slim pruned tool output placeholders so the compaction LLM call is cheaper.
acceptanceCriteria: In session/message-v2.ts toModelMessages(), add stripSynthetic option (default false): when true, skip parts where part.synthetic === true
When stripSynthetic is true and a tool output is already compacted, replace '[output compacted]' with a shorter placeholder: tool name + first 200 chars of input params only
In compaction.ts process(), pass { stripMedia: true, stripSynthetic: true } to toModelMessages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TUI: display cache hit rate below Context indicator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the current session's cache hit rate in the TUI so I can verify caching effectiveness in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: In the TUI component that displays the Context usage indicator, add a line below it showing cache hit rate (e.g. 'Cache: 67%')
Cache hit rate = cumulative cache_read_tokens / (input_tokens + cache_read_tokens + cache_write_tokens) for the current session
Data source: use the existing token tracking from assistant message tokens (no new LLM calls)
When there is no cache data (non-Anthropic provider, or first call), hide the Cache line entirely
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compaction trigger_ratio configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to configure compaction trigger as a ratio of context usage so I can control when the expensive compaction fires.
acceptanceCriteria: In compaction.ts isOverflow(), read config.compaction.buffer (default 20000) and config.compaction.trigger_ratio (optional 0.0-1.0)
If trigger_ratio is set, use threshold = context * trigger_ratio instead of context - buffer
If both trigger_ratio and buffer are set, log a config validation error and prefer trigger_ratio
Add both config entries to config/config.ts schema
Typecheck passes
